_meta.platform_weight: Planned multiplier for reach-weighted scoring. Original intent: weight each source's reach figures by an engagement-quality factor before combining with discourse quality scores in a reach×quality scatter plot (the original Overton-map design). All values are now null — the unit of analysis shifted from whole interventions to individual claims before this weighting was implemented. Original assumed values are preserved per source in platform_weight_note. Future intent: reinstate when reach data is populated and the mapping layer is rebuilt.
_meta.reach_metric: Planned field for per-item reach figures (e.g. view count per video, listener count per episode). Original intent: populate at classification time from the platform API or manual lookup, then multiply by platform_weight to produce a reach score for each intervention. All values are null — data collection was not completed within fellowship timeline. Future intent: automate via YouTube Data API (views, likes) and podcast API equivalents; use as the x-axis of a reach×quality scatter plot to surface high-reach low-quality arguments.

sources.name: Gary Stevenson — YouTube channel
sources.platform_weight_note: Originally 1.0 — baseline weight for YouTube view counts, assumed to represent direct engagement with no completion discount.

sources.name: The Rest Is Politics
sources.platform_weight_note: Originally 0.7 — discounted from baseline to reflect lower assumed completion rates for long-form podcast format versus short YouTube clips. Assumption: podcast listeners may drop off before reaching argument-dense sections.

sources.name: Triggernometry
sources.platform_weight_note: Originally 0.85 — partial discount from baseline; completion estimated at ~60% for long-form YouTube content, offset upward slightly because the format is debate-focused and argument-dense throughout.

sources.name: Novara Media
sources.platform_weight_note: Originally 1.0 — baseline weight, same rationale as gary_stevenson_yt.

sources.name: Hansard (UK Parliament)
sources.reach_type: constituency size x media coverage multiplier
sources.platform_weight_note: Originally 0.5 base weight, intended to be multiplied by a media_coverage_multiplier (1.0–5.0 depending on press pickup). Rationale: direct parliamentary audience is tiny, but statements that generate press coverage reach far larger audiences. The multiplier was never implemented.

sources.name: LBC / Talk TV clips
sources.platform_weight_note: Originally 1.0 — baseline weight; YouTube clip view count used as reach proxy for broadcast content.

sources.name: Rory Stewart — Social media
sources.reach_type: follower count x estimated impression rate
sources.platform_weight_note: Originally 0.9 — near-baseline; Twitter/X follower count used as reach proxy, with impression rate estimated at 15% of followers for viral content. Slight discount from 1.0 to reflect uncertainty in social media reach estimates.
